steps.title: Identify the cylinder volume formula
steps.content:
The volume of a right circular cylinder is the product of its base area and height.
$$V = B \cdot h$$
where $B$ is the base area and $h$ is the height. We're given $V = 432$ cubic centimeters and $B = 24$ square centimeters.

steps.title: Substitute and solve for height
steps.content:
Substituting the known values:
$$432 = 24h$$
Divide both sides by 24:
$$h = \frac{432}{24} = 18$$
The height is 18 centimeters. That matches choice A — A is correct.
